summary: The actual screenshot generally aligns with the wireframe's overall layout and style, including the settings window placement, background map, and basic structure. However, there are discrepancies in content details, button labels, and missing elements like tabs in the wireframe, which affect validation against the screen description. The wireframe is partially suitable but lacks specificity for full requirement validation.

correctElements.evidence: Both actual and wireframe show 'Settings' title at the top of the center-left window, matching the documented title bar requirement.
Actual has settings controls in a bounded area; wireframe shows 'bounded scroll content' placeholder, aligning with the screen description.
Both have a footer with buttons (actual: APPLY/CANCEL; wireframe: BACK/NEXT APPLY), satisfying the fixed footer requirement at a high level.

correctElements.element: Dark starfield background and map elements
correctElements.evidence: Both images feature a dark background with star systems like Sirius and Canopus, thin green borders, and semi-transparent panels.
Both screenshots are at 1536x832, matching the required wireframe and HWND capture dimensions.

incorrectElements.expected: Tabs present as per screen description (e.g., Graphics, Audio, Controls, Server)
incorrectElements.actual: Tabs present in actual but absent in wireframe
incorrectElements.whyIncorrect: Wireframe lacks tabs, making it impossible to validate their presence, positioning, and styling against the description.

incorrectElements.expected: Fixed Apply/Cancel footer as per description
incorrectElements.actual: Wireframe shows BACK and NEXT / APPLY; actual shows APPLY and CANCEL
incorrectElements.whyIncorrect: Mismatch in button text and count prevents accurate validation of user-facing behavior.

incorrectElements.expected: Representative controls like resolution, window mode, UI scale, VSync
incorrectElements.actual: Actual has specific controls; wireframe has generic 'bounded scroll content' text
incorrectElements.whyIncorrect: Wireframe's placeholder does not allow validation of control nesting, text, or functionality.
Text mismatch could indicate inconsistent game state rendering or wireframe inaccuracy.

incorrectElements.expected: Detailed key items as in actual (e.g., System orbit, Stellar body)
incorrectElements.actual: Actual has detailed items; wireframe has empty or minimal 'Map Key' panel
incorrectElements.whyIncorrect: Wireframe lacks content details, limiting validation of persistent UI elements during settings view.

incorrectElements.expected: Visible close button in title bar
incorrectElements.actual: Present and prominent in actual; present but small/subtle in wireframe
incorrectElements.whyIncorrect: Z-order and visibility difference may affect usability validation.

incorrectElements.expected: Pixel-perfect match including DPI and scaling
incorrectElements.actual: Visual inspection shows close match, but no pixel-level diff performed
incorrectElements.whyIncorrect: Audit relies on visual comparison without automated tools, introducing risk of undetected minor bounds or scaling differences.

wireframeSuitability.why: The wireframe captures the basic layout, style, and geometry as a precise baseline, satisfying global requirements and FR-UI-AUDIT-001. However, it uses placeholders instead of specific elements like tabs and controls mentioned in the screen description, limiting its ability to fully validate FR-UI-SCREEN-SETTINGS-WINDOW and user-facing behavior. It does not incorporate repository process requirements from AGENTS-README-FIRST.yaml, such as session logging, but these are not directly relevant to wireframe design.
missingRequirements: Explicit depiction of tabs (Graphics, Audio, Controls, Server)
Specific settings controls (e.g., Resolution dropdown, UI Scale slider, VSync checkbox)
Accurate footer buttons (Apply/Cancel instead of Back/Next Apply)
recommendedWireframeChanges: Add tab strip below title bar with representative tab labels
Replace 'bounded scroll content' placeholder with example settings controls matching the description
Update footer to show Apply and Cancel buttons explicitly
Ensure consistent game state text (e.g., standardize phase name)
